task_summary: SP22b is a fresh smaller visual-polish retry after task #29/session #58 was blocked by issue #21. It keeps work under experiments/mew-ghost only and narrows normal --format human output so the visible product identity is mew-wisp instead of mew-ghost. The compact HUD now presents a resident surface hierarchy, keeps diagnostics behind --details, preserves --form default/cat behavior including centered cat sprite and cat-form state marker placement, and avoids new theme flags, ANSI dependencies, and mew connection work.
proof_artifacts: applied focused test edit and pytest verification tool #1229

verification_evidence.summary: 38 passed after applying the SP22b resident-HUD source and focused test repair.

implemented_contract.fresh_retry_scope: SP22b was implemented as a smaller retry under experiments/mew-ghost only after the previous visual-polish task was blocked by issue #21.
implemented_contract.visible_identity: Normal --format human output now uses a visible mew-wisp product identity in the compact HUD instead of exposing mew-ghost as the resident identity.
implemented_contract.resident_hud_hierarchy: The compact header is 'mew-wisp compact resident HUD' and the leading metadata line is 'resident: mew-wisp | mood: ... | state: ...'. Focus, signal, desk status, and primary action remain compact operator-facing lines.
implemented_contract.diagnostics_stay_behind_details: Freshness, desk primary/details, active-window diagnostics, and launcher intents remain hidden from normal human output and visible only with --details.
implemented_contract.default_and_cat_forms_preserved: The default form keeps the inline marker line using the resident-HUD label, while the cat form keeps the centered cat sprite, cat state line, and existing 'state marker: ...' placement outside the silhouette.
implemented_contract.watch_output_preserved: Human watch output continues to print compact human surfaces for each iteration rather than JSONL records, with assertions updated for the resident HUD and action label.
implemented_contract.state_json_html_live_launcher_preserved: The focused test suite still covers state JSON, HTML output, live-read opt-in gates, and launcher safety behavior.
implemented_contract.scope_limited: No theme flag, ANSI dependency, mew connection work, core src/mew import, or cat.png change was introduced.

covered_cases: desk fixture human output starts with the resident HUD and does not expose the old hud: mew-ghost line
normal human output hides freshness, desk details, active-window diagnostics, launcher intents, HTML, and state JSON
--details human output includes the diagnostic sections while keeping the resident HUD title
bounded and interrupt-style human watch output repeats the resident HUD and action line without JSONL watch metadata
default and cat forms keep their existing behavior while using the new visible HUD identity
cat state marker placement and cat sprite similarity checks remain covered

acceptance_notes: All intended product/test/report changes are scoped under experiments/mew-ghost.
README was not broadened in this repair slice; the user-facing behavior change is covered by focused renderer tests and this local report.
The failed earlier repair was caused by residual old HUD-title assertions; mew repaired those assertions and passed the focused verifier without Codex product-code rescue edits.
remaining_risk: Final git diff/status inspection is still needed after this report write to confirm the exact worktree scope remains experiments/mew-ghost only and cat.png/core files are untouched.
